title: Therapeutic Drug Monitoring: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
nes can often treat conditions that traditional medicine cannot (such as cancer and autoimmune diseases ).
Like with other medicines that need therapeutic monitoring, however, your provider may closely monitor the levels of biologic medicine in your blood to make sure you don't have:
Low levels of the medicine in your blood, which could make the drug less effective. 
High levels of the medicine in your blood, which could make the drug unsafe. 
An immune response to the medicine, which is when your immune system rejects the drug. 
Monoclonal antibodies (mAb) are some of the most common biologic medicines. Laboratories make monoclonal antibodies to only work with specific cells, such as those that are cancerous. Because their targets are so specific, these artificial antibodies can make treatment much more effective. Your provider may monitor mAb levels during treatment for:
Cancer. 
Organ transplant rejection. 
Inflammation and autoimmune diseases. 
Osteoporosis . 
Severe viral infections . 
Nervous system disorders. 
Other examples of biologic medicines include:
Adalimumab. 
Apixaban. 
Nivolumab. 
Pembrolizumab. 
Rituximab. 
Why do I need TDM? 
You may need testing when you first start taking a medicine. This helps your provider figure out the most effective dose for you. Once that dose is determined, you may be tested regularly to make sure the medicine is still effective with
